title: Sorbus minima
taxonomy: Sorbus minima is classified within the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the rose family. This family comprises a diverse range of plants, including roses, apples, pears, and plums. As a member of the Rosaceae family, Sorbus minima is further classified within the genus Sorbus, which includes a variety of mountain ash species. The genus Sorbus is classified within the subfamily Amygdaloideae, which also includes the cherry family. Within the subfamily Amygdaloideae, Sorbus minima is classified as a species of flowering plant.
uses: Sorbus minima is a plant native to China, and its medicinal uses are not well-documented. parts: Sorbus minima, a shrub native to eastern Asia, is known to have medicinal properties. intro: Sorbus minima, also known as the Dwarf Whitebeam, is a small, deciduous shrub or tree native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its white or pink flowers and red berries. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. Sorbus minima contains bioactive constituents such as sorbic acid, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Sorbus minima include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to alleviate symptoms associated with colds, fever, and respiratory issues.
